page: Vultr vhf-8c-32gb is a High Frequency Compute server configured with an Intel x86_64 processor featuring 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores with Hyper-Threading, and a shared CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 512 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. A single complimentary public IPv4 address is bundled with the instance.

In single-core benchmarks, the server demonstrates strong performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU and single-threaded bzip3 decompression scoring in the top 25 percent, and single-threaded gzip compression reaching top-tier status in the top 10 percent. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU tests, falls within the average range. While the Passmark memory score ranks in the top 10 percent, uncached large-block memory bandwidth tests reveal a bottleneck, with read and write speeds categorized in the bottom 25 percent.

The shared CPU allocation presents a performance tradeoff for sustained multi-threaded workloads, as evidenced by poor performance in medium-sized LLM inference tasks. However, the server offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads requiring high single-thread performance and fast local NVMe storage without the premium of dedicated CPU cores. This configuration is well-suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, Redis databases, and lightweight text processing or compression tasks.
description: Vultr vhf-8c-32gb is a High Frequency Compute server featuring an Intel x86_64 architecture with 8 shared vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 32.0 GB of RAM. The server includes 512 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a complimentary public IPv4 address. Benchmark data highlights strong single-core CPU performance and top-tier single-threaded gzip compression, alongside an excellent Passmark memory score. However, multi-core performance remains average, and uncached large-block memory bandwidth is weak. Due to its shared CPU allocation, it is not suited for heavy multi-threaded tasks like medium LLM inference. Qualitatively, this server offers a cost-effective balance of high single-thread speed and fast local storage, making it highly suitable for static web serving, Redis caching, and general-purpose workloads.
og_description: Vultr vhf-8c-32gb is a High Frequency Compute server featuring 8 shared Intel vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 512 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong single-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vhf-8c-32gb features 8 shared Intel vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 512 GB NVMe SSD, offering strong single-core speed for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 server balancing strong single-core CPU performance and fast NVMe storage for general-purpose and web serving workloads.
bullet_points: Intel x86_64 architecture with 8 shared vCPUs and 32 GB RAM
Bundled 512 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and top-tier memory benchmark performance
Tradeoffs in uncached large-block memory bandwidth and multi-core scaling
Suitable for static web serving, Redis, and general-purpose applications
